title: Garlic Rajasthani Beef Curry
description: A flavorful, garlicky Rajasthani-inspired beef curry that pairs tender meat with aromatic spices, cooked quickly in a pressure cooker for a high-protein, light meal.

ingredients.name: Rajasthani spice mix (ground cumin, coriander, red chili, turmeric, garam masala)

instructions: Marinate the beef cubes with yogurt, half of the minced garlic, ginger, and a pinch of salt for 20 minutes.
Heat oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat and sauté the remaining garlic until fragrant.
Add the chopped onions and cook until translucent, then stir in the diced tomatoes and all remaining spices, cooking for 2 minutes.
Place the marinated beef into the cooker, stir to coat with the spice mixture, and add water to cover the meat by about 1 cm.
Seal the cooker and cook on high pressure for 15 minutes; release pressure naturally for 5 minutes before opening.
Open the lid and let the curry simmer on low heat for 5 minutes to thicken, stirring occasionally.
Adjust seasoning with salt and a squeeze of lime juice if desired.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving with steamed rice or flatbread.
